subject: Databases
title: Leaderless Replication
description: A short lesson to help you apply Leaderless Replication.

hook.text: No leader at all. Write to any node. Read from multiple nodes. If enough agree, the value is correct.

buildup.text: Dynamo-style databases (Cassandra, Riak) use leaderless replication. Writes go to W nodes, reads from R nodes.

discovery.text: If W + R > N (total replicas), you're guaranteed to read the latest write. This is a quorum.

twist.text: Leaderless tolerates node failures gracefully. No leader election, no failover. Just reduce the quorum.

climax.text: The trade-off: conflict resolution is needed. Stale data on some nodes must be repaired via read repair or anti-entropy.

punchline.text: Leaderless: write and read anywhere. Quorums ensure correctness.

quiz.question: What does the quorum condition W + R > N guarantee?
options: At least one node in the read set has the latest written value
All nodes always have the same data
Writes are faster because fewer nodes are involved
